rows.notes: Top-of-page title. Very clear handwriting and no amounts present; centered heading 'Soluta ordinaria 1796' is legible.

rows.description: City of Oxford for Sundry Ground Rents
rows.notes: Description legible. Amount column aligned with vertical lines; reads as '4 0 0' (four pounds, 0s, 0d) with clear digits.
Description readable. Amount in columns appears as '0 11 0' (11 shillings). Shillings (11) are within valid range; slight smudge on first digit of pounds but alignment makes it clearly zero.
Description legible. Amount reads '0 1 8' (1s 8d) with clear pence '8' and valid ranges. Vertical alignment unambiguous.
Handwritten description interpreted as 'Church Call to Atkwood' (Atkwood/Atwood). Amount aligned as '0 18 0' (18s). Pounds column faintly blank; shillings '18' clear though slightly slanted.
Abbreviated 'Do' (ditto) then 'to Bennett Sub Treasurer'. Amount reads '0 0 4' (4d). Pence digit '4' clear; pounds and shillings blank/zero by alignment.

College entry 'All souls Coll' is clear. Amount appears to be '4 12 8' (4£ 12s 8d) but the leftmost pounds digit is slightly faded and could plausibly be '10' or '4'; chosen as '4' based on relative width and shape compared with other clear '4's on page. Shillings and pence are legible.

Entry 'Balliol Coll' readable. Amount looks like '10 12 6' (ten pounds 12s 6d). Pounds column digit could be '10' or '4' due to ink/fading; selected '10' because the pounds column shows a taller figure resembling '10' and to match a similar pattern for college entries. Shillings and pence shapes are consistent with '12' and '6'.
Description legible. Amount aligned as '0 8 2' (8s 2d); digits are clear in their columns and within valid ranges.
Long Latin descriptor is readable. Amount '1 14 0' (1£ 14s) is well aligned and the '14' shillings is distinct.
Short descriptor 'Tonsori' (barbers/tonsores) clear. Amount appears as '0 13 4' (13s 4d); digits are distinct and within valid ranges.

Latin heading 'Supervisori Aedificiorum' (Supervisor of buildings) readable. Amount reads '4 0 0' (4£) though the pounds digit is somewhat heavier ink; alignment and spacing make '4' the most plausible reading.
Description 'Janitor' clear. Amount '1 14 6' (1£ 14s 6d) is clearly written with distinct digits; valid ranges.
Latin 'Concionatori' (preachers) legible. Amount appears as '1 0 0' (1£) — pounds digit clear, shillings/pence blank/zero by alignment though pence column very light.
'Pauperibus' (for the poor) description legible. Amount '0 12 0' (12s) clear and in valid range; digits readable though faint.
Description 'Postage Stamps &c' readable. Amount '1 19 1' (1£ 19s 1d) is well aligned; shillings look like '19' and pence '1' is clear.
Description legible. Amount '7 4 0' (7£ 4s) clearly placed in pounds and shillings columns; digits distinct and within valid ranges.
Description readable. Amount reads '3 13 4' (3£ 13s 4d). All three columns visible; shillings 13 and pence 4 clear.
Entry 'House & Window Tax' legible. Amount appears '4 13 6' (4£ 13s 6d); pounds digit slightly faded but matches other '4' shapes on page. Shillings and pence readable.
Description readable. Amount aligned as '1 14 6' (1£ 14s 6d); digits clear and within valid ranges.
Descriptor 'Bedell of the Beggars' legible. Amount '1 3 0' (1£ 3s) clear with distinct digits.

rows.description: Morton Curacy 2 year at L: Day
rows.notes: Handwritten 'Morton Curacy 2 year at L: Day' (interpretation of ordinals and abbreviations). Amount reads '15 0 0' (15£) and the '15' pounds figure is larger and legible, though the description contains abbreviations that are faint.
Description uncertain in parts ('4 year at Miss?' — last word faint/abbreviated). Amount looks like '7 10 0' (7£ 10s) — pounds and shillings are readable though the descriptor is middling legibility.

rows.description: Sir Neive? for rel: 4 year at Xmas
rows.notes: Description is quite faded/abbreviated (interpreted as a name and '4 year at Xmas'). Amount '10 0 0' (10£) is clear, but the wording is uncertain; chosen conservative confidence due to faint text.

rows.description: do ½ year at L: Day
rows.notes: Begins with 'do' (ditto) and a half-year note; description faint. Amount appears again as '10 0 0' (10£). Column alignment clear but descriptive text uncertain, so confidence reduced.
Another 'do' line with abbreviated timing; wording faint and ambiguous. Amount again '10 0 0' (10£) clearly in columns but textual uncertainty lowers confidence.
Third 'do' line referencing a quarter/half-year at 'Michl' (Michaelmas) — description abbreviated. Amount '10 0 0' (10£) appears again; amounts consistent though handwriting for description is faint.
Description 'Corn Driers' legible. Amount '0 3 0' (3s) clear and within valid range; digits distinct.
Entry reads 'Morton Vic: 4 year' (vicarage). Amount '7 10 0' (7£ 10s) visible though the description is slightly abbreviated; digits in amount columns are clear.
Bottom underlined total clearly reads '116 16 7' (116£ 16s 7d). Numerals are distinct and aligned under the pounds/shillings/pence ruling lines; good contrast.
